scenario: A Spring Boot application uses Spring Zeebe to host job workers. The team wants the Spring Boot application to automatically deploy any BPMN/DMN/Form files placed in src/main/resources/processes/ to the Zeebe cluster on startup. Currently each deployment is manual via CLI.
question: Which Spring Zeebe feature enables auto-deployment of process resources?

options.text: bpmn"). Spring Zeebe scans the classpath for resources matching the specified pattern and automatically deploys them to the connected Zeebe cluster during application startup, before any job workers begin polling. The annotation supports multiple resource patterns and wildcard syntax, allowing BPMN, DMN, and Form files to all be included in a single deployment.

join() for each file found. A manual @PostConstruct deployment loop works as an implementation but reinvents the Spring Zeebe SDK's built-in @Deployment annotation convention, adding boilerplate code that must be maintained separately from the declarative deployment mechanism the SDK already provides.

Spring Zeebe does not include any mechanism for automatically deploying BPMN or DMN resources at application startup; teams must use the Camunda Web Modeler API to push files to a Modeler project and trigger deployment from there, then configure the Spring Boot application to connect to the already-deployed process definitions. Spring Zeebe does include the @Deployment annotation specifically for auto-deploying classpath resources at startup. The Web Modeler API is an alternative deployment pipeline, not a workaround for missing Spring Zeebe capability.

xml build file to connect to the Zeebe cluster during the Maven build phase and deploy BPMN/DMN files automatically as part of the mvn install lifecycle, so that every build also pushes the latest process definitions. No camunda-deploy-plugin Maven plugin exists in the Camunda or community ecosystem. Deployment to Zeebe clusters is performed at runtime via the SDK's @Deployment annotation or programmatic API calls, not through Maven build-phase plugins.

a.text: Correct. Spring Zeebe provides the @Deployment annotation (typically placed on the main application class or a configuration class) that triggers auto-deployment of classpath resources matching a pattern on application startup. This eliminates the need for manual deployment steps.

b.text: Incorrect. Manual @PostConstruct works but defeats the purpose of using Spring Zeebe's conventions.

c.text: Incorrect. Spring Zeebe DOES support auto-deployment.

d.text: Incorrect. No such Maven plugin in the Camunda ecosystem.

explanation: Spring Zeebe provides the @Deployment annotation (typically placed on the main application class or a configuration class) that triggers auto-deployment of classpath resources matching a pattern on application startup. This eliminates the need for manual deployment steps.
